revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Isoma Rock Shelter Site]][[ja:磯間岩陰遺跡]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Isoma Rock Shelter Site | native_name =磯間岩陰遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = Isoma Rock Shelter Site A.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Isoma Rock Shelter Site | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Isoma Rock Shelter Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|15.8|N|135|22|59.9|E|region:JP-30|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|qid=Q304266|lt=Tanabe, Wakayama|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = grave | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1969 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Isoma Rock Shelter Site'''|磯間岩陰遺跡 |Isoma iwakage iseki}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with a [[Kofun period]] mass grave located in what is now the Minato neighborhood of city of {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|qid=Q304266|lt=Tanabe|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}} in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163451 |title=磯間岩陰遺跡|trans-title= Isoma Rock Shelter Site |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 1, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located in the innermost part of Tanabe Bay facing the Kii Channel on the western shore of the [[Kii Peninsula]]. The area contains many {{ill|sea cave|en|sea cave|qid=Q1052919|lt=sea cave|1=Sea cave}}s caused by wave erosion, and the Isoma site is a {{ill|Rock shelter|en|Rock shelter|qid=Q1149405|lt=Rock shelter|1=Rock shelter}} measuring about 23 meters wide by about 5 meters deep by 5 meters high on the west slope of a 20-meter hill made of soft sandstone. In 1969, a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, antler products, and human bones, were [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. The largest stone chamber contained the remains of an old man and an infant buried facing each other, and [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s included many fishing implements, including fishing hooks and a harpoon and an iron sword. These remains were covered with the accumulation of fine sand due to the wind erosion of the rock shelter, but there was no evidence that there was a seal on the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], and the remains were originally exposed within the rock shelter. Within the shelter were a total of eight stone burial chambers with an estimated 13 human remains, and it is believed that they were used from the end of the 5th century to the first half of the 7th century AD, based on the contents of the grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Recovered artifacts are exhibited at the Tanabe City Museum of History and Folklore. <ref name="Shiseki"/> These were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1988.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/187602 |title=和歌山県磯間岩陰遺跡出土品|trans-title= Excavated from Isoma Rock Shelter Site, Wakayama Prefecture |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 1, 2021}}</ref>
The site is about 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Kii-Tanabe Station|en|Kii-Tanabe Station|qid=Q2888653|lt=Kii-Tanabe Station|1=Kii-Tanabe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kisei Main Line|en|Kisei Main Line|qid=Q582803|lt=Kisei Main Line|1=Kisei Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|qid=Q6573290|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Isoma Rock Shelter Site}} * [https://www.city.tanabe.lg.jp/bunshin/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai_ichiran.html Tanabe City official site] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Authority control}}
